target: Batched complex FFT (eval_fft/eval_ifft). Bottleneck handed over by rejected bead frankenjax-6lo0c / commit 551eefc4: Complex64/128 were Literal-backed with NO dense slice (unlike as_f64_slice), so eval_fft's extract converted N Literals -> (f64,f64) serially and the output built N complex Literals. Row-threading the transform alone was Amdahl-capped at 1.21x by those serial halves.
lever: Dense Complex storage in fj-core: new LiteralBufferStorage::Complex { values: Arc<Vec<(f64,f64)>>, dtype, literals: lazy } + as_complex_slice() + TensorValue::new_complex_values(). FFT extract now borrows the packed slice (bulk to_vec, no per-Literal match); output is built straight into dense storage. With extract+output now ~free, the per-row transform is ~97% of the work and is fanned out across threads (std::thread::scope, per-thread Bluestein scratch+buf, disjoint output slices, PARALLEL_MIN_ELEMS=1<<18). Complex64 dense buffers are f32-narrowed on construction so the packed slice and the lazily materialized Literal agree bit-for-bit.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT, proven by two committed tests + one golden: (1) dense_complex_input_bit_identical_to_literal_input — dense as_complex_slice input vs Literal input produce identical output bits (fft+ifft, radix-2 len 8 + Bluestein len 6); (2) threaded_batch_fft_matches_per_row_serial — 512x512 (>=1<<18, threaded) batch output equals per-row serial transform bit-for-bit (fft+ifft); (3) fft_golden_output_digest — canonical 64x128 c128 FFT output pinned. Rows are independent (no reordering of any butterfly), so threading is exact.

golden.case: 64x128 complex128 FFT, deterministic input ((i*0.0078125).sin(), (i*0.015625).cos()-0.2), output as LE (re,im) u64-bits stream

measurements_bench_eval_fft_batch_2048x2.notes: Original serial baseline established clean back-to-back this session (stash source, keep bench): 17.83/17.94 ms. dense-input number is same-binary, variance-controlled (cols=256 power-of-two so radix-2 transform is cheap, isolating the storage/threading lever).

score: impact 5 (FFT/IFFT/complex pipelines; dense complex storage is reusable infra for all complex ops) x confidence 5 (bit-exact dense==literal AND threaded==serial, golden pinned, 1093 fj-lax + 130 fj-core tests pass) / effort 2.5 = 10
decision: SHIP. Steady-state (dense complex input, the representation upstream complex ops now produce) is 2.39x; identical-Literal-input path is 1.30x faster with ZERO regression (dense extract fast-path is a no-op for Literal input). Clears Score>=2.0.
tests: fj-lax lib 1093/1093; fj-core lib 130/130; fft module 34/34 (3 new proofs). fft.rs + fj-core changes fmt-clean and clippy-clean (pre-existing einsum.rs:767 type_complexity + foreign lib.rs fmt churn untouched).
follow_ups: eval_rfft/eval_irfft still build Literal output (real/Hermitian paths) — candidate for the same dense treatment. Complex elementwise ops (mul/conj/neg/add) can adopt as_complex_slice for a similar extract win.
